materials_spec: Walnut or cherry hardwood, ¾ inch thick, hand-oiled with Danish oil or tung oil. Playing surface may be inlaid with a contrasting maple or ash panel for the cross-board grid area.
dimensions: Overall board: 10×10 inches ± ¼ inch. Playing grid: 7×7 points at 1¼ inch spacing (7½ inch square grid), centered on board with 1¼ inch border. Board thickness: ¾ inch. 33 shallow drilled point-holes, ¼ inch diameter × ⅛ inch deep.
finish_notes: Grain raised and sanded to 320 grit. Two coats hand-rubbed Danish oil, buffed between coats. Lines burned or laser-engraved — no paint on playing surface. Optional: chamfered or rounded edges, ⅛ inch radius.
pieces_spec: 14 turned wooden pegs: 13 geese in maple or light ash (¼ inch diameter × ¾ inch tall), 1 fox in dark walnut or hand-painted red (same dimensions). Alternatively: hand-turned mushroom-cap pawns, ½ inch base diameter.

materials_spec: Walnut or cherry hardwood, ¾ inch thick, finished with hand-rubbed tung oil. Optional inlay: maple strips set into routed channels for the lines, creating a flush two-tone surface.
dimensions: Overall board: 10×10 inches ± ¼ inch. Outer square: 8×8 inches. Middle square: 5×5 inches. Inner square: 2×2 inches. All squares centered. 24 point positions marked with shallow drilled dimples (⅛ inch diameter × 1/16 inch deep) or brass inlay dots.
finish_notes: Sanded to 320 grit. Hand-rubbed oil finish, 3 coats. Lines routed ⅛ inch wide × 1/16 inch deep and filled with contrasting dark epoxy or wood filler, sanded flush. Optional: branded/laser-engraved maker's mark on underside.
pieces_spec: 18 turned wooden playing pieces: 9 each in two contrasting woods (e.g., walnut and maple). Mushroom-cap pawns, ¾ inch base diameter, ½ inch tall. Or hand-turned discs with chamfered edges.

materials_spec: Dark-stained walnut or mahogany presentation board, ½ inch thick. Denomination zones inlaid or laser-engraved. Token set in turned brass or painted hardwood discs.
dimensions: Board: 10×6 inches ± ¼ inch. Seven denomination zones each 1×4 inches, separated by ⅛ inch routed dividing lines. Storage drawer or fitted box: 4×3 inches.
finish_notes: Sanded to 320 grit, stained dark walnut, hand-rubbed with paste wax. Denomination labels laser-engraved and filled with gold paint. Edges chamfered.
pieces_spec: 49 tokens in 7 groups of 7: laser-engraved or stamped wooden discs, 1 inch diameter, ¼ inch thick. Each denomination group in a distinct wood (or painted in gilt on dark base). Stored in a fitted sliding-lid box.

materials_spec: Hand-stained oak or cherry presentation box with sliding or hinged lid, ¼ inch thick sides. Interior compartment with 3 labeled sections. 100 brass token coins, custom-stamped.
dimensions: Box: 7×5×2 inches ± ¼ inch. Interior divided into 4 sections: 3 explorer compartments + 1 token compartment. Explorer cards: standard 3×5 inches. Tokens: ¾ inch diameter brass discs.
finish_notes: Box exterior stained dark walnut, waxed finish. Interior lined with burgundy felt. Laser-engraved puzzle text on lid or inside of lid. Tokens: antique brass finish, custom-stamped denomination.
pieces_spec: 100 brass-finish metal or turned-wood token coins, ¾ inch diameter. 4 laminated puzzle cards in a kraft-card sleeve. Optional: a brass 'X marks the spot' inlay on the lid.

materials_spec: Cherry or maple hardwood presentation board, ½ inch thick. Nine turned hardwood token discs in a contrasting wood. Board with laser-engraved slot positions and decorative Canterbury Tales-style border.
dimensions: Board: 12×5 inches ± ¼ inch. Nine token positions in a linear arrangement, each 1½ inch diameter circle. Center three positions slightly inset or elevated. Token discs: 1½ inch diameter × ⅜ inch thick.
finish_notes: Board sanded to 320 grit, hand-rubbed tung oil finish. Token position circles laser-engraved or routed ⅛ inch deep. Numerals 1-9 engraved on tokens, filled with black or gold wax. Edges rounded with ⅛ inch router roundover bit.
pieces_spec: 9 turned hardwood discs — alternating walnut and maple for visual distinction. Numbers laser-engraved and filled with contrasting color wax. Stored in a small felt-lined tray at board rear.

commission_title: The Knight's Puzzle Grid Board — Artisan Commission
materials_spec: Dark walnut or ebonized oak board, ¾ inch thick. Brass nails or brass rod pegs set into pre-drilled holes. Optional: heraldic shield shape routed from board edges.
dimensions: Board: 12×12 inches ± ¼ inch. Peg grid: 9×9 array, 1 inch spacing, centered with 1½ inch border. Pegs: ⅛ inch diameter brass rod, ⅜ inch tall above surface. Holes drilled ⅜ inch deep.
finish_notes: Board ebonized or dark-stained, sanded to 320 grit, paste wax finish. Brass pegs polished and press-fit into holes. Optional laser-engraved border with Canterbury Tales heraldic motif.
pieces_spec: 81 brass rod pegs (⅛ inch diameter). Supplied with 12 colored rubber bands and a solution card in a paper envelope. Optional: silk-screened coordinate labels (A-I, 1-9) along board edges.

materials_spec: Alternating walnut and maple inlay squares, ¾ inch thick hardwood border frame. Two corner squares either missing (routed out) or marked with recessed brass X inlays. 31 two-tone wood dominoes.
dimensions: Board: 14×14 inches total. Playing grid: 8×8 squares at 1½ inch each (12×12 inches). Border: 1 inch walnut frame. Thickness: ¾ inch. Dominoes: 3×1½×⅜ inches each.
finish_notes: Walnut and maple squares hand-fitted and glued, sanded flush to 320 grit. Two coats hand-rubbed Danish oil. Domino pieces: walnut on one face, maple on the other, ⅛ inch chamfer on all edges. Solution card printed on archival paper, sealed in a wax envelope.
pieces_spec: 31 two-sided dominoes: ½ walnut, ½ maple (glued down the center), 3×1½ inches. Stored in a fitted mahogany tray that slides under the board. Optional: brass corner brackets on board frame.

materials_spec: 28-tile set in solid cherry or walnut hardwood, each tile 2×1×⅜ inch. Pip holes drilled with Forstner bit and filled with contrasting wood plugs (maple pips on walnut tiles, or walnut pips on maple). Bone-line divider inlaid or routed.
dimensions: Each tile: 2 inches × 1 inch × ⅜ inch (standard proportions). Pip holes: 3/16 inch diameter × ⅛ inch deep. Storage box: 6×4×1½ inches.
finish_notes: Tiles sanded to 320 grit. Three coats hand-rubbed Danish oil, buffed between coats. Pip holes filled with contrasting-wood turned pegs (glued flush and sanded). Bone line: routed ⅛ inch wide × 1/16 inch deep, filled with white or ebony wood filler. Storage box: matched wood with sliding lid or brass clasp.
pieces_spec: 28 tiles. Optional: brass spinner in center of each tile (traditional style). Storage in a fitted sliding-lid box in matching wood with felt-lined interior.

materials_spec: Alternating walnut and maple wood strip inlay board, ¾ inch thick, with cherry or walnut border frame. 24 turned hardwood checker discs in walnut and maple.
dimensions: Overall board: 16×16 inches. Playing grid: 8×8 squares at 1¾ inch each (14 inch playing area). Border frame: 1 inch wide. Thickness: ¾ inch. Checker discs: 1¾ inch diameter × ¼ inch thick.
finish_notes: Wood-inlay squares sanded flush to 320 grit. Two coats hand-rubbed Danish oil. Border frame with mitered corners, ⅛ inch roundover on top edge. Checker discs: turned, chamfered edge, hand-oiled. Optional: monogram or date laser-engraved on board underside.
pieces_spec: 24 turned hardwood checker discs: 12 walnut, 12 maple. Each 1¾ inch diameter, ¼ inch thick, chamfered edge. Stored in two felt bags tied with ribbon. Optional: sliding drawer built into board frame for piece storage.

materials_spec: Strip-and-crosscut walnut/maple inlay chessboard, ¾ inch thick, with figured walnut or rosewood border frame. Playing squares 2¼ inches. No paint — pure wood color contrast.
dimensions: Overall board: 20×20 inches. Playing grid: 8×8 squares at 2¼ inch each (18 inch playing area). Border frame: 1 inch walnut or rosewood. Thickness: ¾ inch. Optional coordinate labels inlaid in frame.
finish_notes: Strip-and-crosscut method: ¼ inch wide alternating strips of walnut and maple glued, sliced, and re-glued in offset pattern. Sanded flush to 320 grit. Three coats hand-rubbed Danish oil, wet-sanded between coats. Frame mitered at corners with spline reinforcement. Bottom felt-lined.
pieces_spec: Board only (pieces sourced separately — recommend Staunton pattern in ebonized boxwood and natural boxwood). Optional: sliding drawer in board frame for storage. Alternatively, we can commission a full turned-wood Staunton set in walnut and maple.

materials_spec: Alternating walnut and maple or light/dark veneer inlay on a ¾ inch maple substrate. Corner camp boundaries inlaid with contrasting wood strips or brass rule. 4-color set of wooden cone pawns.
dimensions: Overall board: 22×22 inches. Playing grid: 16×16 squares at 1¼ inch each (20 inch playing area). Border: 1 inch walnut frame. Camp boundary: 4 triangular areas marked with inlaid contrasting border. Piece pawns: ½ inch base diameter, ¾ inch tall cones.
finish_notes: Inlay squares sanded flush to 320 grit. Two coats hand-rubbed Danish oil. Camp zones highlighted with ⅛ inch inlaid maple or ebony rule. Border frame mitered corners. Board underside felt-lined. Optional: player's initials laser-engraved at each camp.
pieces_spec: For 2-player: 38 turned wooden cones (19 each in 2 contrasting wood types). For 4-player: 52 turned wooden cones (13 each in 4 colors: natural maple, walnut, cherry, ebonized oak). Stored in a fitted divided tray.

materials_spec: Alternating walnut and maple inlay 10×10 board, ¾ inch thick, with walnut border frame. Optional engraved coordinate labels for tournament use. 40 turned hardwood checker discs.
dimensions: Overall board: 18×18 inches. Playing grid: 10×10 squares at 1½ inch each (15 inch playing area). Border: 1½ inch walnut frame. Thickness: ¾ inch. Checker discs: 1¼ inch diameter × ¼ inch thick.
finish_notes: Inlay squares sanded flush to 320 grit. Hand-rubbed Danish oil. Border frame mitered corners with spline reinforcement. Felt underside. Optional: coordinate numbers engraved per International Draughts notation (1-50 on dark squares).
pieces_spec: 40 turned hardwood checker discs: 20 walnut, 20 maple. Each 1¼ inch diameter × ¼ inch thick with chamfered edges. Stored in a fitted box. Note: checker discs should be 75-80% of square size so they fit comfortably.

materials_spec: Dark green leather or felt playing surface mounted on ½ inch MDF or maple substrate, with walnut or cherry hardwood border frame. 64 two-sided discs in ebonized maple (black side) and natural maple (white side).
dimensions: Overall board: 14×14 inches. Playing grid: 8×8 squares at 1½ inch each (12 inch playing area). Border frame: 1 inch walnut. Thickness: ¾ inch. Disc diameter: 1⅜ inch. Recessed wells: ⅛ inch deep.
finish_notes: Border frame walnut, hand-oiled, mitered corners. Playing surface: green baize or leather fitted inside frame, secured with contact cement. Grid lines: white or gold silk-screened on felt. Disc wells: 64 recessed circles ⅛ inch deep, routed with 1⅜ inch Forstner bit. Discs: lathe-turned, chamfered, 1 face ebonized, 1 face natural maple.
pieces_spec: 64 turned maple discs, 1⅜ inch diameter × ⅜ inch thick. One face ebonized (Japan black or black dye), other face natural maple oil finish. Chamfered edges. Stored in two felt pouches inside a sliding-lid box built into board base.

materials_spec: Natural wood-slice board (cross-section of a branch, 8-10 inch diameter), live edge, hand-sanded and oiled. Grid lines wood-burned. Playing pieces: smooth river stones or turned walnut and maple discs.
dimensions: Board: 8–10 inch diameter wood slice, ¾ inch thick. Grid: two burned lines in each direction, dividing board into 9 equal zones. Pieces: 5 smooth stones per player (2-3 inch stones), or 5 turned discs per player (1½ inch diameter × ½ inch thick).
finish_notes: Live-edge board: bark edge preserved or cleaned, natural oil finish (2-3 coats Danish oil). Grid lines: wood-burned ⅛ inch wide. Stone pieces: selected, sanded smooth if needed, one set painted with X (white paint), one set painted with O (black paint), sealed with clear coat. Alternatively, turned wood discs in contrasting walnut and maple.
pieces_spec: 10 smooth river stones or turned hardwood discs, matched in size. 5 labeled X, 5 labeled O. Stored in a small cloth bag attached to board with a jute cord.

commission_title: Fox and Geese (Peg Board) — Artisan Commission
materials_spec: Walnut or cherry hardwood board, ¾ inch thick, with 33 drilled holes. Turned wooden pegs in two contrasting woods. Board optionally shaped to the cross outline (outer edge routed to the cross shape).
dimensions: Board: 9×9 inches (square) or cross-shaped cutout. 33 holes in cross pattern: 7×7 grid minus 4 corner 2×2 groups, at 1¼ inch spacing. Hole diameter: ¼ inch, ½ inch deep. Pegs: ¼ inch diameter dowel, ¾ inch tall above surface.
finish_notes: Board sanded to 320 grit, hand-oiled with 2 coats Danish oil. Lines between holes engraved or burned. Cross outline edge optionally routed with ¼ inch roundover. Pegs turned to a mushroom-cap head for easier grip.
pieces_spec: 14 turned wood pegs: 13 maple or ash (geese), 1 cherry or rosewood (fox). Each ¼ inch diameter, ¾ inch above surface, with ¼ inch peg that slots into hole. Stored in a small drilled storage strip on board underside.

materials_spec: Green baize playing surface on a ¾ inch MDF or hardwood substrate, 18×12 inches. Six numbered zones silk-screened or painted in gilt on green. Brass birdcage tumbler with 3 engraved dice. Hardwood frame.
dimensions: Layout: 18×12 inches. Six betting zones each 2½×4 inches in a row. Border: 1 inch hardwood or leather frame. Optional cage: 4-inch diameter wire birdcage tumbler on a walnut stand.
finish_notes: Baize surface fitted over substrate, tacked under frame. Numbers in gold silk-screen or hand-painted gilt. Frame in stained walnut or mahogany with brass corner pieces. Cage: brass wire or copper-plated wire frame on turned walnut axle stand.
pieces_spec: 3 large dice (1 inch), engraved with standard pip pattern. 60 betting tokens: antique-finish brass discs, ¾ inch diameter. Optional: full Victorian birdcage tumbler in brass on a walnut stand.

materials_spec: Compact folding chess board in walnut and maple inlay, ½ inch thick when folded. Letterpress-printed notation cards on cream laid stock. Optional: magnetic travel chess pieces.
dimensions: Folding board: 8×8 inches unfolded (4×8 inches folded). 8×8 playing grid at 1-inch squares. Notation cards: 5×3 inches, 30-row format, 20 cards per set. Storage: fitted leather or book-cloth portfolio.
finish_notes: Folding board: piano hinge in brass, walnut frame, inlay squares sanded flush. Hand-rubbed oil finish. Coordinate labels inlaid or laser-engraved in frame. Notation cards: letterpress or laser-printed on 100lb cream cardstock with monogram or family crest option.
pieces_spec: Optional: 32 magnetic Staunton-style chessmen in turned boxwood (natural and ebonized), with magnetic bases. Stored in a velvet-lined tray inside the folded board. Portfolio: bookcloth-covered, with pocket for cards and envelopes.

commission_title: The Game of Logic — Artisan Commission
materials_spec: Two inlaid hardwood diagram boards (biliteral and triliteral), each 5×5 inches, in contrasting wood with routed cell divisions. 9 turned hardwood counters in red-stained and grey-stained maple.
dimensions: Biliteral board: 5×5 inches, 2×2 grid with 2-inch cells. Triliteral board: 5×5 inches, 8-cell grid (2×4 zones with inner squares). Board thickness: ½ inch. Counters: ¾ inch diameter × ¼ inch thick.
finish_notes: Boards in light maple with routed ⅛ inch wide cell division lines filled with dark stain. Labels engraved (x, x', y, y', m, m'). Hand-rubbed oil finish. Counters: 4 dyed red, 5 dyed grey, all sealed with clear lacquer. Stored in a felt-lined box with a copy of Carroll's original puzzle card set.
pieces_spec: 9 turned maple counters: 4 red (¾ inch diameter), 5 grey (¾ inch diameter). Two diagram boards. Puzzle cards: 20 Carroll syllogisms printed on cream cardstock, stored in a silk envelope. Optional: facsimile of the 1887 first edition included.

materials_spec: CNC-routed or laser-cut hardwood hexagonal board, 12 inches flat-to-flat, ¾ inch thick, mounted in a circular walnut frame. Six concentric rings painted in traditional spectrum colors or stained in natural wood tones. 14 turned hardwood pieces.
dimensions: Board: 12 inches flat-to-flat (13.9 inches corner to corner), ¾ inch thick. 91 hexagonal spaces at 1¾ inch flat-to-flat each. 6 concentric rings. Border: 1 inch walnut ring frame. Queen pieces: 1¼ inch diameter disc, ½ inch tall. Guard pieces: 1 inch diameter disc, ⅜ inch tall.
finish_notes: Hexagonal cells routed ⅛ inch deep or silk-screened with color fills. Ring colors traditional (6-spectrum) or in natural wood tones (ebony, walnut, cherry, maple, ash, bleached). Board sanded to 320 grit, hand-oiled. Circular walnut frame with hand-cut joints. Pieces turned, chamfered, hand-oiled in two contrasting woods.
pieces_spec: 14 turned hardwood pieces: 2 queen discs (1¼ inch, ½ inch tall, in contrasting woods or with crown motif), 12 guard discs (1 inch, ⅜ inch tall). Stored in a felt-lined divided box. Optional: laser-engraved crown symbol on queen pieces.

materials_spec: Three-board nested set in walnut, cherry, and maple respectively — one board per Morris variant. Each board with routed or inlaid connecting lines. 36 turned wooden playing pieces in 4 contrasting woods.
dimensions: Board 1 (Three Men's Morris): 7×7 inches, 2-square grid. Board 2 (Six Men's Morris): 9×9 inches, 2-square grid. Board 3 (Nine Men's Morris): 11×11 inches, 3-square grid. All boards ¾ inch thick. Pieces: ¾ inch diameter discs, ¼ inch thick, 9 each in 4 woods.
finish_notes: Each board in a distinct wood species. Lines routed ⅛ inch wide × 1/16 inch deep, filled with contrasting dark filler. Point positions marked with ⅛ inch brass inlay dots. Boards hand-oiled. Pieces in 4 contrasting woods: walnut, maple, cherry, ebonized oak.
pieces_spec: 36 turned discs: 9 each in walnut, maple, cherry, ebonized oak. Stored in a 4-compartment felt-lined tray. Three boards stored flat in a fitted cloth envelope or thin wooden case.

materials_spec: Walnut and maple alternating inlay 8×8 chessboard, ¾ inch thick, with walnut border frame. 64 turned maple tokens numbered 1-64 (laser-engraved). One hand-carved or turned walnut knight piece.
dimensions: Board: 14×14 inches overall. Playing grid: 8×8 squares at 1½ inch each (12 inch playing area). Border: 1 inch walnut frame. Board thickness: ¾ inch. Numbered tokens: ¾ inch diameter × ¼ inch thick discs. Knight piece: standard Staunton scale (~2½ inch tall).
finish_notes: Inlay squares sanded flush to 320 grit. Hand-rubbed Danish oil, two coats. Tokens in natural maple, numerals laser-engraved and filled with black wax. Coordinate labels (a-h, 1-8) engraved in border frame. Knight piece: hand-carved walnut or turned + carved, hand-oiled.
pieces_spec: 64 numbered maple token discs (laser-engraved 1-64). 1 walnut knight piece (hand-carved or lathe-turned). Solution card on archival cream paper, sealed in wax envelope. Stored in a felt-lined divided tray with sliding lid.

materials_spec: American black walnut base (¾" thick, bookmatched grain preferred); turned cherry or maple pegs (⅜" diameter with decorative tenon at base); seven graduated discs in alternating cherry and maple, ¼" thick
dimensions: Base: 13" × 4" × ¾". Pegs: 8" tall × ⅜" diameter. Discs: 1½" to 3" diameter in ¼" steps, ¼" thick. Overall assembled height: approx. 9".
finish_notes: Three coats of hand-rubbed tung oil on base and pegs, satin finish. Discs finished with beeswax polish for smooth sliding. Laser-engraved move-count table on underside of base (127 moves for 7 discs).
pieces_spec: 1 walnut base with 3 brass-bushed peg sockets; 3 turned pegs; 7 graduated discs (4 cherry, 3 maple in alternating pattern); felt feet on base underside

materials_spec: English oak base with burned grid pattern; turned beech pegs; eight discs in two alternating hardwoods (oak and ash), ¼" thick
dimensions: Base: 15" × 4" × ¾". Pegs: 8" tall × ⅜" diameter. Discs: 1¼" to 3" diameter in ¼" steps, ¼" thick.
finish_notes: Fumed oak finish on base for aged appearance; hand-rubbed linseed oil on all pieces; laser-engraved title 'The Reve's Puzzle — Canterbury, 1907' on base front edge.
pieces_spec: 1 oak base with 4 brass-bushed peg sockets; 4 turned pegs; 8 graduated discs (4 oak, 4 ash in alternating pattern); green baize base lining

materials_spec: 3–4 oz vegetable-tanned saddle leather, natural tan; one antique brass 4-hole button, 1¼" diameter; edges burnished and waxed
dimensions: Leather strip: 9" × 1½" × 3–4 oz weight (approx. 1.5mm thick). Slits: two parallel cuts ¾" long, ⅜" apart, centered at midpoint. Button: minimum 1¼" diameter.
finish_notes: Edges beveled with edge tool and hand-burnished with bone folder; light coat of neatsfoot oil; optional blind-tooled border around slit area for decorative framing.
pieces_spec: 1 leather strip (pre-cut slits); 1 antique brass button; presented in a small kraft envelope with solving instructions on reverse

commission_title: Cups and Balls Set — Parlour Edition
materials_spec: Three cups turned from American black walnut; four balls hand-felted from Merino wool roving; velvet-lined mahogany presentation box
dimensions: Cups: 3¼" tall × 2¾" diameter at mouth × 2" diameter at top (inverted). Wall thickness: ¼". Balls: 1" diameter felted Merino wool. Box: 12" × 4" × 4".
finish_notes: Three coats of hand-rubbed tung oil, satin finish; concave top depression for ball placement; decorative beaded rim at mouth; matched grain pattern across all three cups where possible.
pieces_spec: 3 turned walnut cups (nested set, matching dimensions); 4 hand-felted Merino wool balls in deep crimson; green baize cup-roll performance mat; silk drawstring pouch; illustrated performance routine card

materials_spec: Twelve cards letterpress-printed on 300gsm cream card stock; ninety tokens turned from hornbeam or bone (or laser-cut from ¼" maple); velvet-lined octagonal mahogany box with brass clasp
dimensions: Cards: 7" × 4" each, with printed grid. Tokens: ¾" diameter × ¼" thick wooden discs, numbers burned or engraved. Markers: ½" mother-of-pearl or brass discs (15 per player color × 4 colors = 60 markers). Box: 10" × 8" × 3".
finish_notes: Cards letterpress-printed with Victorian engraving-style border; box interior lined in green or burgundy baize with fitted card tray and token well; box exterior in dark-stained mahogany with French polish finish.
pieces_spec: 12 player cards; 90 numbered draw tokens; 60 player markers (15 per player, 4 colors); velvet draw bag with brass ring closure; rule card printed on parchment

materials_spec: Mahogany base board, ¼" thick; brass rail strips (2mm × 1mm section) for track; engine and carriages turned or carved from cherry; hand-lettered brass plates for each carriage
dimensions: Base: 14" × 10" × ¼". Track rail height: 2mm above base. Engine block: 1½" × ¾" × ¾". Carriage blocks: 1" × ¾" × ½" each.
finish_notes: Base finished with mahogany stain and satin lacquer; brass rails pinned with brass panel pins; engine painted gloss black with red detailing; carriages painted in four period railway liveries (GWR green, Midland Railway red, LNWR black, Caledonian blue) with hand-lettered running numbers.
pieces_spec: 1 mahogany base with brass track; 1 engine piece; 4 carriage pieces; puzzle card with starting position and challenge; velvet-lined storage tray

materials_spec: Frame in American cherry, ¾" thick × 1" wide; tiles in bird's-eye maple, ¼" thick, with laser-engraved numerals in medieval-style serif font; base in cherry-veneered plywood
dimensions: Frame exterior: 10½" × 10½" × 1¼". Interior tray: 9¾" × 9¾". Tiles: 3" × 3" × ¼" each (8 numbered tiles). Clearance: ⅛" total gap per axis for smooth sliding.
finish_notes: Frame finished with three coats hand-rubbed Danish oil; tiles finished with wax polish for ultra-smooth sliding; numerals laser-engraved and filled with black India ink; felt pads on frame underside.
pieces_spec: 1 cherry frame with plywood back; 8 bird's-eye maple numbered tiles; storage sleeve (puzzle ships scrambled); solution card in envelope

commission_title: Four Frogs Puzzle — Lily Pond Edition
materials_spec: Nine lily pad discs hand-carved from lime wood (3" diameter, slightly concave surface); four frogs hand-carved and painted from boxwood; base rail in walnut
dimensions: Lily pads: 3" diameter × ½" thick, slightly dished surface. Frog pieces: 1¼" × 1" × ¾" carved form. Base rail: 20" × 2" × ½" walnut, with 9 shallow recesses to hold lily pads.
finish_notes: Lily pads hand-painted in two shades of green with oil paint, white flower detail, satin-varnished; frogs painted in naturalistic coloring; walnut base oiled and waxed; pieces store in a linen bag.
pieces_spec: 9 carved lime wood lily pads; 2 green frog pieces; 2 brown frog pieces; walnut base rail with position recesses; linen storage bag; solution card

materials_spec: 12 sheets premium cold-press watercolor paper (Arches or Fabriano, 140 lb); set of 3 artist-grade white wax resist sticks; set of 6 quality watercolor half-pans in reveal-optimized colors; hand-bound booklet with instructions
dimensions: Paper: 9" × 12" per sheet. Kit box: 10" × 6" × 2". Includes 3 pre-drawn reveal papers as demonstration pieces.
finish_notes: Three sample papers pre-drawn with progressively complex images (simple shape, landscape, portrait); illustrated instruction booklet with historical notes printed on aged-appearance paper; papers stored flat between tissue.
pieces_spec: 12 blank watercolor paper sheets; 3 pre-drawn reveal demonstration sheets; 3 white wax resist sticks; 6-color watercolor set; 2 brushes (sizes 6 and 10); instruction booklet; wooden presentation box

commission_title: Knight's Tour Puzzle Board — Squire's Edition
materials_spec: Maple and American black walnut strip-built board (¾" squares); hand-carved knight in boxwood; solid walnut frame with mitered corners; backed with cherry plywood
dimensions: Board playing surface: 8" × 8" (¾" per square). Frame: adds 1" on each side, total 10" × 10". Thickness with frame: ¾". Knight piece: approx. 2¼" tall standard chess proportions.
finish_notes: Board surface leveled with hand plane and scraped to glass-smooth finish; three coats tung oil; frame stained dark walnut with satin lacquer; carved knight in natural boxwood finish, hand-rubbed with carnauba wax.
pieces_spec: 1 strip-built maple/walnut chess board in walnut frame; 1 hand-carved boxwood knight; laminated solution card showing Euler's famous closed knight's tour; velvet storage pouch for knight

materials_spec: Solid maple, ½" thick, with painted and lacquered chessboard surface; pieces sawn on a precision scroll saw; walnut shadow-box tray
dimensions: Board/puzzle assembled: 9" × 9" × ½". Tray: 9½" × 9½" × ¾" walnut, French-fitted with thin lining. 13 individual cut pieces.
finish_notes: Chessboard surface painted with artist-grade acrylic, 6 coats lacquer for durability; cut edges beveled 1° to allow easy pickup; tray in walnut with oil finish; solved-state reference photo included.
pieces_spec: 13 precisely cut maple puzzle pieces; walnut display/storage tray; laminated reference photo of solved state; booklet with Dudeney's original commentary

materials_spec: Maple and American walnut strip-built board (¾" squares); hand-turned rook in cherry; solid walnut frame with mitered corners
dimensions: Board surface: 8" × 8" (¾" per square). Frame adds 1" each side, total 10" × 10". Rook: approx. 1¾" tall, 1" base diameter.
finish_notes: Surface hand-leveled and scraped smooth; three coats tung oil; frame in dark-stained walnut; dry-erase laminate overlay included so players can mark tours without marking the board.
pieces_spec: 1 strip-built chessboard in walnut frame; 1 turned cherry rook; dry-erase overlay sheet with fine-tip dry-erase marker; solution card with 16-turn minimum tour

materials_spec: Maple and walnut strip-built board; 14 hand-turned bishops in cherry (matching set); walnut frame
dimensions: Board: 8" × 8" playing surface in walnut frame (10" × 10" total). Bishop pieces: 2" tall × 1" base diameter, standard proportions.
finish_notes: Board finished as per Knight's Tour build; bishops in natural cherry finish, hand-rubbed with carnauba wax; each bishop given a concave base to prevent tipping on wood surface.
pieces_spec: 1 strip-built chessboard in walnut frame; 14 matching turned cherry bishops; storage felt-lined tray; challenge card with problem statement and solution

commission_title: Eight Queens Puzzle Board — Dudeney Edition
materials_spec: Maple and walnut strip-built board; 8 hand-turned queens in cherry; walnut frame; playing surface optionally scored with algebraic notation
dimensions: Board: 8" × 8" surface in 10" × 10" walnut frame. Queen pieces: 2¾" tall × 1" base diameter.
finish_notes: Board as per Knight's Tour build; queens in cherry with Danish oil and carnauba wax finish; bases felt-lined for smooth placement without scratching board; algebraic notation laser-engraved on border.
pieces_spec: 1 strip-built chessboard in walnut frame; 8 matching turned cherry queens; storage tray; hint card and list of all 12 fundamental solutions

materials_spec: Maple and cherry strip-built 5×5 board; hand-carved knight in boxwood; presentation case in walnut
dimensions: Board: 4¾" × 4¾" playing surface (¾" per square) in a 6" × 6" walnut frame. Knight: approximately 1¾" tall, scaled to ¾" square proportion. Case: 7" × 7" × 1" walnut.
finish_notes: Board surface scraped and polished to satin finish; three coats tung oil on frame; knight in natural boxwood waxed; case with magnetic closure and green baize lining.
pieces_spec: 1 strip-built 5×5 puzzle board in walnut frame; 1 carved boxwood knight; folded solution card showing all 25 squares numbered; walnut presentation case
